title: Pouteria rubriflora
taxonomy: Pouteria rubriflora is a species of plant that belongs to the family Sapotaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants in the order Ebenales. The Sapotaceae family is a group of tropical plants that includes various species of fruit trees and other woody plants. Pouteria rubriflora is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Its classification also places it within the larger clade of angiosperms, the flowering plants.

intro: Pouteria rubriflora, also known as the Red-flowered Pouteria, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria rubriflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pouteria rubriflora include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, wounds, and gastrointestinal disorders.
